channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: # I Built a Legal OS for a Zimbabwean Law Firm — Here's What AI-Assisted Legal Research Actually Looks Like
item.description: My wife is a lawyer and partner at a law firm in Harare, Zimbabwe. She has been practising for over two decades, juggles her caseload with speaking engagements, and runs legal education programs...

item.title: enable AI's Full Potential: Structure Your Codebase for Agent Success
item.description: Cursor and Claude Code can take a 200-component design system and ship a working admin page without you writing a line. That's not marketing — I've watched it happen on an OTF SaaS kit. The catch...

item.title: Stop Putting Everything in useState
item.description: There's a pattern I see constantly: every piece of UI state gets its own useState, the component re-renders on every interaction, someone adds useMemo to fix the performance, and the whole thing...

item.title: Reskilling Will Not Save Us
item.description: In February 2026, Mustafa Suleyman, the chief executive of Microsoft AI, told the Financial Times that artificial intelligence would achieve “human-level performance on most, if not all,...

item.title: AI Coding Tip 026 - Assign a Persona to Every Skill Definition
item.description: Know who speaks before the skill runs TL;DR: Always define a clear role at the top of every skill file so you know whose perspective drives the execution. Common Mistake ❌ You write a skill full...

item.title: Are you ready for what it takes to stop ghost guns?
item.description: In the summer of 2024, former Army National Guard member Andrew Scott Hastings spent a sweaty afternoon carefully packing boxes with parts he made using his 3D printer. These weren't novelty...

item.title: LayerNorm vs BatchNorm: why Transformers normalize per token, not per batch
item.description: 📏 Play with the LayerNorm vs BatchNorm visualizer: https://dev48v.infy.uk/dl/day27-layer-norm.html Normalization inside a neural net is almost embarrassingly simple: take some numbers, subtract...

item.title: CI/CD Security Tools — The Complete Guide for Engineering Teams
item.description: CI/CD security tools help engineering teams catch vulnerable dependencies, leaked secrets, insecure code patterns, risky containers, and cloud misconfigurations before they reach production. The...

item.title: How collaborative filtering recommends from ratings alone
item.description: Here is the thing that still feels a little magical to me about recommender systems: a good one can suggest a film in a genre you have never touched, and be right, without knowing a single thing...

item.title: The Duck Stops Here: Building hid_guard — Catching Keystroke Injection at the Kernel Level
item.description: This is the index post for a series on building hid_guard, a HID-BPF security monitor for the Linux kernel — from the first embarrassingly bad prototype to an RFC on the linux-input mailing list....

item.title: Recognition tooling for engineering teams (and why we built our own)
item.description: The morale problem nobody puts in the sprint board Distributed engineering teams have a blind spot. You can see build times, PR throughput, incident counts, and cycle time on a dashboard. You...

item.title: Google Is Suing Chinese Scammers Who Are Using Gemini
item.description: Not sure this will have any effect, but I support the effort: According to Google’s legal filing, Outsider Enterprise operates through Telegram. The group offers phishing-as-a-service to...

item.title: Nothing’s first B-series phone is also skipping the US
item.description: Nothing is shaking up the branding of its cheapest phones, following up last year's Phone 3A Lite with a new Phone 4B. Combining design elements of the 4A and 4A Pro, it follows Nothing's previous...

item.title: Nothing’s new earbuds can record calls and what you’re listening to
item.description: Nothing announced a new pair of budget-friendly wireless earbuds, but not as part of the company's affordability-focused sub-brand, CMF. The new Nothing Ear 3A are available starting today through...

item.title: Why worms (and microbes) are catching on as a manure pollution solution
item.description: Anthony Agueda, a third-generation California dairy farmer, pulls a rake through a bed of dark, wet wood chips on his family’s land in Hickman, a tiny town in the state’s agricultural heartland....

item.title: Erling Haaland Is Everywhere at the World Cup. Most of It Is AI
item.description: Norwegian striker Erling Haaland isn’t just a footballer anymore. He’s become an internet character perpetuated by fans and AI.

item.title: British Space Startup Launches Longevity Lab Into Orbit
item.description: The lab will beam back data to train AI models to predict how proteins behind age-related diseases like Alzheimer’s and certain cancers behave.

item.title: Suspected China-Aligned Hackers Exploit Roundcube Flaws Against Universities
item.description: A suspected China-aligned threat activity cluster has been observed exploiting Roundcube webmail software belonging to physics and engineering departments of U.S. and Canadian universities as part...

item.title: The first American autonomous ground vehicles are fighting in Ukraine
item.description: Forterra has deployed more than 100

item.title: CERT/CC Warns of Hidden Admin Backdoor in Tenda Router Firmware
item.description: Several versions of firmware released by Chinese network device manufacturer Tenda have been found to embed an undocumented authentication backdoor that enables administrative access to the...

item.title: BeyondTrust Patches Critical Auth Bypass Flaws in Remote Support and PRA
item.description: BeyondTrust has released updates to address two critical security flaws affecting Remote Support (RS) and Privileged Remote Access (PRA) products that, if successfully exploited, could allow...

item.title: LeRobot v0.6.0: Imagine, Evaluate, Improve
The ‘first’ AI-run ransomware attack still needed a human
item.description: An AI agent carried out the technical execution of a real-world ransomware attack for the first known time, but new details show a human still chose the victim, set up the infrastructure, and...

item.title: US investors will soon get access to SK Hynix, another memory maker riding the AI boom
item.description: SK Hynix is experiencing a boom credited to AI. It will ride that to a multibillion-dollar U.S. IPO, expected to take place on Friday.

item.title: Hoto’s PixelDrive screwdriver is down to $60, matching its best price
item.description: If your Prime Day purchases included a new desk, TV stand, bookshelf, or other furniture you still haven’t assembled, Hoto’s PixelDrive cordless screwdriver can help speed up the process. It’s...

item.title: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch on the fight to split off models from agents
item.description: "The reality is, when you're optimizing for production, you start looking at a price/performance," Guillermo Rauch tells TechCrunch.

item.title: You can now customize Siri’s pace and expressivity in the latest iOS 27 beta
item.description: The update is part of Apple's broader effort to make Siri feel more natural and personal, as it rebuilds the assistant around generative AI.

item.title: America’s greatest idea is still under threat
item.description: The United States of America recently turned 250 years old. What a spectacle! The fireworks were amazing, and millions of proud people celebrated across the nation - even around the world. France...

item.title: Every major tech layoff in 2026 that has name-checked AI
item.description: A running look — in reverse chronological order — at the bigger tech companies that have announced significant layoffs this year with AI as a stated factor.

item.title: Iran-Linked Hackers Use New Cavern C2 Framework to Target Israeli Organizations
item.description: An Iranian hacking group affiliated with Iran's Ministry of Intelligence and Security (MOIS) has been wielding a previously undocumented modular command-and-control (C2) framework dubbed Cavern...
